pi_items.verified_value: CSV with 6 columns, 20 data rows plus header. Inconsistent capitalization, date formats (MM/DD/YYYY, 'Month YYYY', 'Best by MM/YYYY', 'No expiration'), and location/category names
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates home_inventory_messy.csv with specified data

pi_items.verified_value: Data uses hardcoded historical dates that become increasingly outdated. README claims '~3-4' expired items, but CSV contains 19 expired items as of task execution date 2026-03-07
pi_items.source: CSV expiration dates range from 2023-04 to 2025-12; README expectations appear calibrated for 2024 execution timeframe

privileged_info_summary: The task contains a synthetic dataset with 20 items in CSV format with intentionally messy formatting (inconsistent locations, categories, date formats). When executed on 2026-03-07, the hardcoded expiration dates result in 19 expired items and 1 good item (no expiration). Task verification requires 8 criteria with 75% pass threshold (6/8). Timeout is 300 seconds with 30 max steps. CRITICAL FINDING: README claims '~3-4' expired items and '~2-3' urgent items, but actual data yields 19 expired, 0 urgent when processed as of 2026-03-07. This contradiction suggests the dataset dates are stale relative to documented expectations.
